skill.name: arrange
skill.description: Improve layout, spacing, and visual rhythm. Fixes monotonous grids, inconsistent spacing, and weak visual hierarchy to create intentional compositions.
skill.summary: Transform generic layouts into intentional, rhythmic compositions through systematic spacing and visual hierarchy improvements.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 29 potential issues but all are false positives. The skill file contains only markdown documentation with CSS code snippets for design guidance. No executable code, external commands, or security risks present. All findings result from pattern matching on documentation text (CSS property names, design terminology) rather than actual code execution.

content.user_title: Transform Layouts with Intentional Spacing and Rhythm
content.value_statement: Eliminate monotonous grids and arbitrary spacing. This skill provides a systematic approach to create visual hierarchy through intentional spacing, varied rhythm, and structured layouts that guide the eye to what matters.
actual_capabilities: Analyze existing layouts for spacing consistency and visual hierarchy issues
Design systematic spacing scales using framework tokens or custom properties
Apply CSS Flexbox and Grid appropriately for 1D and 2D layouts
Create visual rhythm through intentional tight and generous spacing
Break card grid monotony with varied compositions and asymmetric layouts
Establish semantic z-index and shadow scales for depth management
limitations: Requires design context gathering first via frontend-design skill for best results
Cannot fix layouts without access to the actual CSS or styling code
Focuses on spatial design rather than color or typography decisions
May need manual verification across different screen sizes for responsiveness

use_cases.title: Fix monotonous card grids
use_cases.description: Transform repetitive icon-heading-text card layouts into varied, visually interesting compositions with proper rhythm
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developer working on dashboard or marketing pages

use_cases.title: Establish design system spacing
use_cases.description: Create consistent spacing scales and documentation for a component library or design system
use_cases.target_user: Design systems engineer building reusable components

use_cases.title: Improve visual hierarchy
use_cases.description: Apply squint test and spacing principles to make primary content obvious within 2 seconds
use_cases.target_user: UI designer refining landing page or product interface

prompt_templates.title: Quick layout assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess the layout of TARGET=components/Hero.tsx for spacing issues, visual hierarchy, and rhythm problems
prompt_templates.scenario: Get a quick analysis of layout problems in a specific component or file

prompt_templates.title: Fix spacing system
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply a consistent spacing scale to TARGET=pages/dashboard.tsx using the framework's built-in tokens. Fix any arbitrary spacing values.
prompt_templates.scenario: Replace random padding/margin values with a systematic spacing scale

prompt_templates.title: Break card grid monotony
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign the features section in TARGET=sections/Features.tsx to avoid repetitive card layouts. Create visual variety through asymmetric compositions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Transform a monotonous card grid into an engaging, varied layout

prompt_templates.title: Full layout overhaul
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full arrange skill on TARGET=. Start with context gathering, then assess the current layout, plan systematic improvements, and implement changes for better visual rhythm and hierarchy.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive layout redesign following the complete skill workflow

output_examples.input: Assess the layout of the pricing section - it feels crowded and monotonous
output_examples.output: I identified three layout issues: (1) Equal 16px spacing throughout creates no rhythm - apply tight 8px for related elements and 64px between sections, (2) All cards are identical size - vary spans to create emphasis, (3) Everything centered creates static feel - use left-aligned content with asymmetric accent placement

output_examples.input: Fix the spacing system in our dashboard
output_examples.output: Replaced 23 arbitrary spacing values with systematic tokens: xs=4px, sm=8px, md=16px, lg=24px, xl=32px, 2xl=48px, 3xl=64px. Applied 8px gaps within card content, 24px between dashboard widgets, 64px between page sections. Layout now has clear rhythmic breathing room.

best_practices: Always run frontend-design skill first to gather design context before making layout changes
Use the squint test - blur your vision to verify hierarchy is clear without detail
Apply gap for sibling spacing instead of margins to avoid collapse complications
Start with Flexbox for 1D layouts, use Grid only when you need true 2D control
anti_patterns: Do not use arbitrary spacing values - always work from a defined scale
Do not make all spacing equal - variety between tight and generous creates hierarchy
Do not default to card grids for everything - spacing and alignment can group content naturally
Do not center everything - left-aligned compositions with asymmetry feel more designed

faq.question: Do I need to run other skills first?
faq.answer: Yes, run frontend-design skill first. It provides design principles and the Context Gathering Protocol that arrange requires.

faq.question: Will this skill change my colors or fonts?
faq.answer: No, arrange focuses only on layout, spacing, and visual hierarchy. It does not modify colors or typography.

faq.question: Can I use this with any CSS framework?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill works with Tailwind, CSS-in-JS, custom CSS, or any framework. Use your framework's spacing scale or define custom tokens.

faq.question: What is the squint test?
faq.answer: The squint test means viewing your layout with blurred vision. If you can still identify the most important elements and groupings, your hierarchy works.

faq.question: Should I use Flexbox or Grid?
faq.answer: Use Flexbox for 1D layouts like nav bars or button groups. Use Grid for 2D layouts like dashboards where rows and columns need coordination.

faq.question: How do I fix monotonous card grids?
faq.answer: Vary card sizes, span columns, mix cards with non-card content, or use spacing alone to create grouping without card containers.
